cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Limit notation: the limit of $f(x)$ as $x$ approaches $c$ equals $L$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Left-hand (one-sided) limit notation as $x$ approaches $c$ from the left

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Right-hand (one-sided) limit notation as $x$ approaches $c$ from the right

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Condition for a two-sided limit to exist (in terms of one-sided limits)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Three conditions for continuity of $f$ at $x = c$ (list all three, separated by commas)
cards.answer: f(c) \text{ is defined},\ \lim_{x \to c} f(x) \text{ exists},\ \lim_{x \to c} f(x) = f(c)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Fundamental trigonometric limit: $\dfrac{\sin x}{x}$ as $x \to 0$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Fundamental trigonometric limit: $\dfrac{1 - \cos x}{x}$ as $x \to 0$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Squeeze Theorem: if $g(x) \leq f(x) \leq h(x)$ near $c$ and $\lim_{x \to c} g(x) = \lim_{x \to c} h(x) = L$, then...

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Intermediate Value Theorem (IVT): if $f$ is continuous on $[a, b]$ and $k$ is between $f(a)$ and $f(b)$, then there exists $c$ such that...
cards.answer: f(c) = k \text{ for some } c \in (a, b)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Limit at positive infinity notation

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Infinite limit notation: $f(x)$ increases without bound as $x$ approaches $c$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Limit of a sum (sum law of limits)
cards.answer: \lim_{x \to c} [f(x) + g(x)] = \lim_{x \to c} f(x) + \lim_{x \to c} g(x)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Limit of a product (product law of limits)
cards.answer: \lim_{x \to c} [f(x) \cdot g(x)] = \lim_{x \to c} f(x) \cdot \lim_{x \to c} g(x)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Limit of a quotient (quotient law of limits)
cards.answer: \lim_{x \to c} \frac{f(x)}{g(x)} = \frac{\lim_{x \to c} f(x)}{\lim_{x \to c} g(x)}, \quad \lim_{x \to c} g(x) \neq 0

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Limit of a constant multiple (constant multiple law of limits)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Horizontal asymptote definition (using limit notation at $+\infty$)
cards.answer: y = L \text{ is a horizontal asymptote if } \lim_{x \to +\infty} f(x) = L

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Vertical asymptote definition (using one-sided infinite limit notation at $x = c$)
cards.answer: x = c \text{ is a vertical asymptote if } \lim_{x \to c^\pm} f(x) = \pm\infty

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: A discontinuity where $\lim_{x \to c} f(x)$ exists but either $f(c)$ is undefined or $f(c) \neq \lim_{x \to c} f(x)$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: A discontinuity where $\lim_{x \to c^-} f(x) \neq \lim_{x \to c^+} f(x)$, so both one-sided limits exist but are unequal

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: A discontinuity where at least one one-sided limit is $\pm\infty$ (e.g., a vertical asymptote within the domain)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: The theorem that guarantees a value $c \in (a,b)$ with $f(c) = k$ whenever $f$ is continuous on $[a,b]$ and $k$ is between $f(a)$ and $f(b)$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: The theorem used to evaluate limits of functions that are bounded between two functions sharing the same limit

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: A function $f$ is said to have this property at every point in its domain if it has no breaks, holes, or jumps

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: A function is described as this type of continuous when it is continuous on its entire domain (e.g., polynomials, sine, cosine, exponentials)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Indeterminate form produced when direct substitution gives $\dfrac{0}{0}$ or $\dfrac{\infty}{\infty}$ — write the symbolic form

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Limit of a rational function $\dfrac{p(x)}{q(x)}$ as $x \to \infty$ when $\deg(p) < \deg(q)$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Limit of a rational function $\dfrac{p(x)}{q(x)}$ as $x \to \infty$ when $\deg(p) = \deg(q)$ (with leading coefficients $a$ and $b$)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Limit of a rational function $\dfrac{p(x)}{q(x)}$ as $x \to \infty$ when $\deg(p) > \deg(q)$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: The algebraic technique used to evaluate limits of the form $\dfrac{\sqrt{f(x)} - a}{g(x)}$ by multiplying numerator and denominator by the conjugate expression

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: The property of a function $f$ such that $\lim_{x \to c} f(g(x)) = f\!\left(\lim_{x \to c} g(x)\right)$ — the limit can be 'passed inside'

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Squeeze Theorem applied to $x^2 \sin\!\left(\dfrac{1}{x}\right)$ as $x \to 0$: write the bounding inequality used

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Natural exponential limit: value of $\left(1 + \dfrac{1}{n}\right)^n$ as $n \to \infty$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: A point $c$ in the domain of $f$ where $f$ is continuous from the right ($\lim_{x \to c^+} f(x) = f(c)$) but NOT from the left
cards.answer: right-endpoint continuity (or: continuous from the right at $c$)

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: A condition on a closed interval $[a,b]$ where $f$ is continuous on $(a,b)$, $\lim_{x \to a^+} f(x)=f(a)$, and $\lim_{x \to b^-} f(x)=f(b)$

cards.subject: ap-calculus-bc
cards.prompt: Limit definition of the derivative at a point (difference quotient form)
